task: There is a bug in src/utils.js: parseDate() returns NaN for valid ISO date strings like '2026-06-14'. Find the cause and fix it.

input.query: parseDate ISO date string NaN bug in src/utils.js

input.learning: parseDate() in src/utils.js parses ISO date strings (YYYY-MM-DD) by splitting on '-'. A prior version split on '/', which caused Number(parts[i]) to return NaN for ISO inputs like '2026-06-14'. Fix: use String(s).split('-') and pass year/month-1/day to the Date constructor.
input.title: parseDate must split ISO date strings on '-' not '/'
input.evidence: src/utils.js:1-4 now splits on '-' so parseDate('2026-06-14') returns a valid timestamp instead of NaN.
